newsSince: No new fundamental catalyst since June 9. The FY2026 order forecast (raised to JPY170-220B, but below heightened expectations) and the operating-profit hike to JPY100B were the early-June earnings already captured in the June 9 case; the +28% move since then is a sector/flow rebound. AI/2nm supercycle and EUV mask-blank inspection monopoly remain intact.

sources.label: Lasertec order outlook disappoints despite profit hike (BigGo)
Lasertec revises order outlook upward on AI demand (Futu)

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node lens: EUV mask-blank inspection monopoly and 2nm/High-NA WFE thesis unchanged since June 9. Order outlook raise was below expectations but already in the score; +28% since June 9 is flow, not new evidence. Hold.
verdicts.bull: 100% share in EUV photomask-blank inspection; order backlog near record on 2nm/High-NA migration through 2030.
verdicts.neutral: Order forecast raised but below heightened expectations; cyclical FY26 sales dip already known.
verdicts.bear: Lumpy order timing, export-control risk, and a steep 20%+ FY26 sales decline could disappoint a crowded book.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: High-NA EUV mask inspection ramp / order-book re-acceleration into CY26-27.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Order timing volatility and China export-control exposure.

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO/metrology lens: Lasertec is an indirect inspection play, not co-packaged optics. No new window evidence; +28% since June 9 marginally erodes residual asymmetry on a heavily-covered former retail darling. Hold with slight negative drift.
verdicts.bull: Scarce, monopoly metrology gate to advanced lithography; indispensable to leading-edge yield.
verdicts.neutral: Only indirectly tied to CPO; richly owned and well covered.
verdicts.bear: +28% no-news rebound consumes mispricing on this crowding-sensitive list.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Advanced-node inspection demand from foundry capex.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Semicap spending volatility and crowded positioning.
